findings.name: Subscription has excessive Owner role assignments
findings.description: 12 principals hold the Owner role at subscription scope. Excessive Owner assignments increase the blast radius of a compromised identity and violate least-privilege.
findings.recommendation: Reduce Owner role assignments to 3 or fewer. Use Contributor or custom roles with scoped permissions. Enable PIM for just-in-time elevation.

findings.name: Custom RBAC role with wildcard action permissions
findings.description: Custom role 'DevOps-FullAccess' includes action '*/write' which grants write access to all resource types, effectively replicating Contributor privileges.
findings.recommendation: Replace wildcard actions with specific resource provider actions. Review and narrow the custom role definition.

findings.name: Guest users with elevated roles in Azure AD
findings.description: 4 guest users have been assigned Contributor or higher roles. Guest accounts from external tenants with elevated privileges pose a lateral movement risk.
findings.recommendation: Review guest user role assignments. Restrict guest access to specific resource groups. Implement access reviews for guest accounts.

findings.name: Multi-factor authentication not enforced for all administrators
findings.description: Conditional Access policies do not enforce MFA for all users with administrative roles. 3 Global Administrators and 5 Privileged Role Administrators lack MFA enforcement.
findings.recommendation: Create a Conditional Access policy requiring MFA for all directory roles. Enable Security Defaults or configure per-user MFA for all admins.

findings.name: Network Security Group allows unrestricted SSH access
findings.description: NSG 'prod-backend-nsg' has inbound rule allowing TCP/22 from source 0.0.0.0/0. Unrestricted SSH access exposes VMs to brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks.
findings.recommendation: Restrict SSH access to known management IP ranges or use Azure Bastion for secure VM access. Remove the 0.0.0.0/0 source rule.

findings.name: Network Security Group allows unrestricted RDP access
findings.description: NSG 'windows-mgmt-nsg' has inbound rule allowing TCP/3389 from source 0.0.0.0/0. Publicly exposed RDP is the top vector for ransomware initial access.
findings.recommendation: Remove the public RDP rule. Use Azure Bastion or a VPN gateway. If RDP is required, restrict to specific IP addresses with NSG rules.

findings.name: Network Security Group has overly permissive outbound rules
findings.description: NSG 'app-tier-nsg' allows all outbound traffic on all ports (0.0.0.0/0:*). This enables unrestricted data exfiltration and command-and-control communications.
findings.recommendation: Implement egress filtering. Define explicit outbound rules allowing only required destinations and ports. Deny all other outbound traffic.

findings.name: Storage account allows public blob access
findings.description: Storage account 'proddata2024sa' has AllowBlobPublicAccess set to true. Containers configured with public access can expose sensitive data to the internet.
findings.recommendation: Set AllowBlobPublicAccess to false at the storage account level. Use SAS tokens or Azure AD authentication for authorized access.

findings.name: Storage account not using customer-managed encryption keys
findings.description: Storage account 'hrfilestorage' uses Microsoft-managed keys for encryption. Customer-managed keys (CMK) in Key Vault provide greater control over key lifecycle.
findings.recommendation: Enable customer-managed keys using Azure Key Vault. Configure automatic key rotation with a 90-day rotation policy.

findings.name: Storage account allows access from all networks
findings.description: Storage account 'logsarchivesa' has network rules set to 'Allow' from all networks. Any public IP can attempt to access the storage endpoints.
findings.recommendation: Configure storage firewall rules to allow access only from specific VNets and IP ranges. Use private endpoints for production workloads.

findings.name: Key Vault soft delete not enabled
findings.description: Key Vault 'app-secrets-kv' does not have soft delete enabled. Accidental or malicious deletion of secrets/keys results in permanent data loss.
findings.recommendation: Enable soft delete and purge protection on all Key Vaults. Set the retention period to at least 90 days.

findings.name: Key Vault secrets without expiration dates
findings.description: 17 secrets in Key Vault 'app-secrets-kv' have no expiration date set. Long-lived secrets increase the window of exposure if compromised.
findings.recommendation: Set expiration dates on all secrets. Implement automated rotation using Azure Key Vault rotation policies or Azure Functions.

findings.name: Key Vault access not restricted to private endpoints
findings.description: Key Vault 'prod-credentials-kv' is accessible via public endpoint. Public access to credential stores expands the attack surface.
findings.recommendation: Disable public network access. Configure private endpoints for Key Vault access. Ensure DNS resolution routes to the private IP.

findings.name: Azure SQL Server allows connections from all Azure services
findings.description: SQL Server 'prod-sql-server' has 'Allow Azure services and resources to access this server' enabled. This permits any Azure-hosted service to connect.
findings.recommendation: Disable the 'Allow Azure services' firewall rule. Use VNet service endpoints or private endpoints for application connectivity.

findings.name: Azure SQL Database transparent data encryption using service-managed keys
findings.description: SQL Database 'customer-db' uses service-managed TDE keys. Customer-managed keys provide control over the encryption key lifecycle.
findings.recommendation: Configure TDE with customer-managed keys stored in Azure Key Vault. Enable automatic key rotation.

findings.name: SQL auditing not enabled
findings.description: SQL Server 'staging-sql-server' does not have auditing enabled. Without auditing, security-relevant database events go unrecorded.
findings.recommendation: Enable SQL auditing and direct audit logs to a storage account or Log Analytics workspace. Set retention to at least 90 days.

findings.name: App Service does not enforce HTTPS only
findings.description: App Service 'api-gateway-app' accepts HTTP connections. Unencrypted HTTP traffic exposes authentication tokens and sensitive data in transit.
findings.recommendation: Enable 'HTTPS Only' on the App Service. Configure HTTP to HTTPS redirection at the application level.

findings.name: App Service using outdated TLS version
findings.description: App Service 'legacy-portal-app' allows TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 connections. These protocols have known vulnerabilities including POODLE and BEAST.
findings.recommendation: Set minimum TLS version to 1.2 on all App Services. Test client compatibility before enforcement.

findings.name: App Service managed identity not configured
findings.description: App Service 'data-processor-app' does not use a managed identity. Application secrets stored in code or configuration are vulnerable to exposure.
findings.recommendation: Enable system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity. Use the identity to authenticate to Azure resources without storing credentials.

findings.name: AKS cluster running without Azure Policy add-on
findings.description: AKS cluster 'prod-aks-cluster' does not have the Azure Policy add-on enabled. Without policy enforcement, workloads can run with elevated privileges.
findings.recommendation: Enable the Azure Policy add-on for AKS. Apply built-in policies for pod security, network policies, and resource limits.

findings.name: AKS cluster API server accessible from public internet
findings.description: AKS cluster 'prod-aks-cluster' has a public API server endpoint with no authorized IP ranges configured. The Kubernetes API is accessible from any IP.
findings.recommendation: Configure authorized IP ranges for the AKS API server. For maximum security, deploy a private AKS cluster.

findings.name: Activity Log alerts not configured for critical operations
findings.description: No Activity Log alerts are configured for security-critical operations such as policy assignment changes, NSG modifications, or Key Vault access policy changes.
findings.recommendation: Create Activity Log alerts for: policy assignment changes, NSG rule modifications, Key Vault access policy updates, and role assignment changes.

findings.name: Diagnostic settings not enabled for all subscriptions
findings.description: 2 of 4 subscriptions do not have diagnostic settings configured. Activity logs from these subscriptions are not forwarded to a central Log Analytics workspace.
findings.recommendation: Enable diagnostic settings on all subscriptions. Forward Activity Logs to a central Log Analytics workspace for SIEM integration.

findings.name: Microsoft Defender for Cloud not enabled on all resource types
findings.description: Defender plans are disabled for: Key Vaults, DNS, Resource Manager, and Storage. These resources lack advanced threat detection and security alerts.
findings.recommendation: Enable Defender for Cloud plans on all resource types. At minimum enable for Servers, SQL, Storage, Key Vault, and Containers.

findings.name: Security contacts not configured for Defender alerts
findings.description: No email addresses or phone numbers are configured for Defender for Cloud security alert notifications. Critical alerts may go unnoticed.
findings.recommendation: Configure security contact email addresses and enable notification for high-severity alerts. Add a security distribution list.

findings.name: Virtual Network without DDoS Protection
findings.description: VNet 'prod-vnet' does not have Azure DDoS Protection Standard enabled. Basic DDoS protection may be insufficient for production workloads.
findings.recommendation: Enable Azure DDoS Protection Standard on production VNets. Configure DDoS alerts and telemetry.

findings.name: No Network Watcher flow logs configured
findings.description: NSG flow logs are not enabled for 6 NSGs in the production resource group. Network traffic analysis and forensics capabilities are limited.
findings.recommendation: Enable NSG flow logs for all production NSGs. Configure Traffic Analytics for enhanced visibility. Set log retention to 90 days.

findings.name: Storage account secure transfer not enforced
findings.description: Storage account 'devtestfiles' does not enforce secure transfer (HTTPS). Data in transit could be intercepted via unencrypted HTTP connections.
findings.recommendation: Enable 'Secure transfer required' on all storage accounts to enforce HTTPS for all REST API operations.

findings.name: No Privileged Identity Management configured for eligible roles
findings.description: Azure AD PIM is not configured for eligible role assignments. All privileged role assignments are permanent rather than time-bound.
findings.recommendation: Configure Azure AD PIM for just-in-time role activation. Set maximum activation duration to 8 hours. Require approval for critical roles.

findings.name: Azure Security Benchmark compliance score below 70%
findings.description: Current Azure Security Benchmark compliance score is 58%. 34 recommendations are outstanding across compute, networking, and identity controls.
findings.recommendation: Review and remediate outstanding Azure Security Benchmark recommendations. Prioritize critical and high-severity items first.

findings.name: Resource locks not applied to critical resources
findings.description: Production resource groups and critical resources (SQL servers, Key Vaults) do not have CanNotDelete or ReadOnly locks. Accidental deletion is possible.
findings.recommendation: Apply CanNotDelete locks to production resource groups and critical resources. Use ReadOnly locks for immutable infrastructure.
